slashCommands.name: specify
slashCommands.description: Create an AI-refined BrainGrid requirement from a vague idea

params.prompt:
Use the BrainGrid CLI to turn the user's idea into a detailed, structured requirement.

Steps:
1. If $input is provided, use it as the prompt. Otherwise ask the user for their idea.
2. Run: braingrid specify --prompt "<user's idea>"
3. Show the created requirement ID and name.
4. Ask if the user wants to break it down into tasks now.

If CLI is not installed: npm install -g @braingrid/cli
If not authenticated: braingrid login
If no project: braingrid init

Output formats: Use --format markdown for full content.

slashCommands.name: breakdown
slashCommands.description: Break a BrainGrid requirement into implementation tasks

params.prompt:
Break down a BrainGrid requirement into AI-ready implementation tasks.

Steps:
1. Get requirement ID from $input (accepts REQ-123, req-123, or 123).
   If not provided, auto-detect from git branch (feature/REQ-X-description).
   If auto-detection fails, ask the user.
2. Run: braingrid requirement breakdown <REQ-ID>
3. Show the tasks created.
4. Suggest creating a git branch: git checkout -b feature/REQ-<id>-<slug>
5. Suggest running /build to get the full implementation plan.

Handle errors reactively: install CLI if missing, login if not authenticated.

slashCommands.name: build
slashCommands.description: Fetch the complete BrainGrid implementation plan with all task prompts

params.prompt:
Fetch a BrainGrid requirement's complete implementation plan.

Steps:
1. Get requirement ID from $input (accepts REQ-123, req-123, or 123).
   If not provided, auto-detect from git branch (feature/REQ-X-description).
   If auto-detection fails, ask the user.
2. Run: braingrid requirement show <REQ-ID> --format markdown
3. Display the full output: requirement details + all task prompts.
4. Show task count and summary.
5. Suggest starting implementation with the first task.
6. Update requirement status: braingrid requirement update <REQ-ID> --status IN_PROGRESS

If additional instructions are in $input after the requirement ID, acknowledge them and apply to the implementation plan.

Handle errors reactively.

slashCommands.name: save-requirement
slashCommands.description: Save the current discussed plan as a BrainGrid requirement

params.prompt:
Save the current conversation's plan as a structured BrainGrid requirement.

Steps:
1. Review the conversation to understand what feature/plan was discussed.
2. Get a title from $input, or derive one from the discussion.
3. Summarize the full discussed plan into a detailed description.
4. Run: braingrid requirement create --name "<title>" --content "<full description>"
5. Show the created REQ-ID.
6. Ask if the user wants to break it down: braingrid requirement breakdown REQ-X

Handle errors reactively.

contextProviders.name: braingrid
contextProviders.description: Fetch the current BrainGrid requirement and task context
